title: hydrangea hortensis var. rosalba
intro: Hydrangea hortensis var. rosalba, also known as Bigleaf Hydrangea or White Hydrangea, is a type of flowering plant species that belongs to the Hydrangea genus. Traditionally, the roots of Hydrangea hortensis var. rosalba have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, relieving menstrual cramps, and reducing fever, due to its ability to act as a diuretic and calm the nervous system. Hydrangea hortensis var. rosalba can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for its medicinal uses, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions with other medications.
